templates.title: Send polished follow-ups right after every call
templates.description: As soon as a transcript arrives, this coworker builds a short recap, drafts the email, and creates the matching CRM follow-up task.
templates.triggerDescription: When an Aircall call transcription becomes available.
agentInstructions: Get call details with aircall_get_call using your Aircall connection ID.
Get transcription with aircall_get_transcription using your Aircall connection ID.
Extract the external participant phone number from number.raw_digits.
Search HubSpot contacts by phone with hubspot_search_contacts and request properties: email, firstname, lastname.
If contact payload is incomplete, call hubspot_get_contact to fill missing fields.
Generate a 2-3 sentence call summary and explicit action items for both parties.
If contact email exists, create a Gmail draft with friendly greeting, short summary, bullet action items, and professional closing.
Create a HubSpot task with subject 'Follow up on call with [Contact Name]', include summary + actions, and schedule for tomorrow at 9 AM.
If contact exists, associate task to contact using HUBSPOT_DEFINED association type 204.
If no contact is found, skip Gmail draft and still create the HubSpot task with the phone number in the body.

summaryBlocks.title: Capture new call transcript
summaryBlocks.body: Watches for a completed transcript and pulls metadata for the contact context.

summaryBlocks.title: Find the matching CRM contact
summaryBlocks.body: Searches HubSpot by phone and enriches the lead record before writing the follow-up.

summaryBlocks.title: Draft the follow-up in one shot
summaryBlocks.body: Writes concise notes and creates both a Gmail draft and CRM task.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Transcript ready] --> B[Fetch transcript and call metadata]
    B --> C[Lookup contact in HubSpot]
    C --> D[Generate follow-up summary]
    D --> E[Create Gmail draft]
    D --> F[Create HubSpot task]

templates.title: Turn your company list into finance leads ready to call
templates.description: Process one row at a time from Google Sheets, find the best finance decision-maker, enrich contact data, and push to CRM.
templates.triggerDescription: Run manually on an unprocessed company row from your spreadsheet.
agentInstructions: Read the selected company row from Google Sheets and capture company name, website, country, and any notes.
Research the company to identify the best finance decision-maker for outbound outreach.
Use LinkedIn data to validate the person, title, seniority, and company fit.
Return one primary contact and one backup contact when available.
Normalize the contact name, title, LinkedIn URL, and confidence notes.
Upsert the contact into HubSpot and attach the source company row reference.
Write enrichment status and the selected contact back to the spreadsheet.

summaryBlocks.title: Read the next target company
summaryBlocks.body: Loads one spreadsheet row at a time so the workflow stays reviewable and deterministic.

summaryBlocks.title: Find the finance buyer
summaryBlocks.body: Researches likely economic buyers and validates them against the company profile.

summaryBlocks.title: Write the lead into CRM
summaryBlocks.body: Pushes the selected contact to HubSpot and marks the row as processed.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Manual row selection] --> B[Read company row]
    B --> C[Research finance leadership]
    C --> D[Validate best contact]
    D --> E[Upsert contact in HubSpot]
    E --> F[Update sheet status]

templates.title: Qualify every new Calendly booking before the meeting
templates.description: When a new meeting is booked, this coworker researches the person and company, enriches the CRM, and sends a concise briefing.
templates.triggerDescription: When a qualifying meeting lands on your calendar from Calendly.
agentInstructions: Watch for new sales meetings added to the connected calendar.
Extract attendee name, email domain, company clues, and meeting owner.
Research the person and company using LinkedIn and public context.
Check HubSpot for existing contact and company records before creating anything new.
Create or update the CRM contact with role, seniority, and fit notes.
Produce a short briefing with who they are, why they may be buying, and what to ask.
Send the briefing to the assigned rep in Slack before the meeting.

summaryBlocks.title: Capture the meeting context
summaryBlocks.body: Pulls attendees and timing from the booked calendar event.

summaryBlocks.title: Research the attendee
summaryBlocks.body: Builds a lightweight qualification brief from person and company context.

summaryBlocks.title: Send the rep a prep note
summaryBlocks.body: Posts a concise Slack briefing so the rep walks into the call informed.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Meeting booked] --> B[Load event details]
    B --> C[Research attendee and company]
    C --> D[Update CRM context]
    D --> E[Draft briefing]
    E --> F[Send to Slack]

templates.title: Map every decision-maker in your top deals automatically
templates.description: Every Monday morning, this coworker scans emails, Slack, call notes, and CRM data across your key deals to identify missing stakeholders.
templates.triggerDescription: Run each Monday morning for open strategic deals.
agentInstructions: List active high-value deals from Salesforce or HubSpot based on stage and amount.
Review recent emails, call summaries, and Slack mentions related to each deal.
Identify named stakeholders, blockers, and likely missing decision-makers.
Research missing stakeholders on LinkedIn and match them to the target account.
Write stakeholder recommendations and confidence notes back to the CRM.
Post a short digest of the riskiest stakeholder gaps to Slack.

summaryBlocks.title: Scan your key deals
summaryBlocks.body: Starts from a filtered set of high-priority opportunities.

summaryBlocks.title: Find stakeholder gaps
summaryBlocks.body: Combines recent communication signals with account research to spot missing buyers.

summaryBlocks.title: Push insights back to the team
summaryBlocks.body: Updates CRM notes and flags the riskiest deals for the account team.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Weekly schedule] --> B[Load priority deals]
    B --> C[Scan comms and notes]
    C --> D[Identify missing stakeholders]
    D --> E[Research on LinkedIn]
    E --> F[Update CRM and Slack]

templates.title: Know who you meet in your next sales call
templates.description: Walk into every call already knowing who they are and what they care about. Let your coworker do the homework you never have time to finish.
templates.triggerDescription: Run shortly before the next sales call on your calendar.
agentInstructions: Find the next qualifying sales meeting on the connected calendar.
Extract attendee names, domains, role hints, and internal owner.
Research each attendee and company using LinkedIn and public context.
Summarize likely goals, risks, and recent company signals.
Prepare a short meeting brief with suggested talk tracks and questions.
Send the brief to Slack for the meeting owner.

summaryBlocks.title: Watch the upcoming calendar
summaryBlocks.body: Targets the next relevant meeting so prep stays timely.

summaryBlocks.title: Research the people in the room
summaryBlocks.body: Builds fast context around attendee role, company, and likely agenda.

summaryBlocks.title: Deliver a focused brief
summaryBlocks.body: Sends a short prep note with questions and likely objections.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Before next meeting] --> B[Load attendee list]
    B --> C[Research people and company]
    C --> D[Prepare talk track]
    D --> E[Send meeting brief]

templates.title: Turn every call transcript into CRM-ready deal intelligence
templates.description: This coworker polls CloudTalk, extracts structured commercial insight from each transcript, and logs clean records in your CRM.
templates.triggerDescription: When a new CloudTalk call transcript is finalized.
agentInstructions: Capture the call transcript and metadata when the call provider marks it complete.
Extract summary, pains, buying signals, objections, next steps, and competitor mentions.
Match the call to the right contact, company, and open opportunity in the CRM.
Write a structured note or activity entry back to the CRM.
Flag calls with high urgency, risk, or clear follow-up gaps.
Send an exception alert to Slack when the CRM match is missing.

summaryBlocks.title: Ingest the transcript
summaryBlocks.body: Receives the finished transcript together with the call metadata.

summaryBlocks.title: Extract commercial insight
summaryBlocks.body: Normalizes the transcript into CRM-friendly sales signals.

summaryBlocks.title: Sync and escalate exceptions
summaryBlocks.body: Logs the note in CRM and alerts Slack if a clean match is missing.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Transcript complete] --> B[Parse transcript]
    B --> C[Extract deal signals]
    C --> D[Match CRM record]
    D --> E[Write structured note]
    D --> F[Alert Slack on mismatch]

templates.title: Capture closed-lost lessons before they get buried
templates.description: When a HubSpot deal moves to closed-lost, this coworker reviews deal activity and writes a reusable company-level loss brief.
templates.triggerDescription: When a closed-lost signal is received from the CRM or revenue ops inbox.
agentInstructions: Watch for a closed-lost status change on an opportunity.
Review deal notes, emails, stage history, and associated tasks.
Extract likely loss reasons, internal misses, and competitive patterns.
Write a concise loss summary that can be reused across similar accounts.
Store the insight on the account or opportunity record.
Post a short lesson summary to Slack for the team.

summaryBlocks.title: Detect the loss event
summaryBlocks.body: Starts when a deal officially moves to closed-lost.

summaryBlocks.title: Review what happened
summaryBlocks.body: Pulls together notes and context to identify why the deal was lost.

summaryBlocks.title: Share the lesson
summaryBlocks.body: Captures a reusable loss brief and posts the headline takeaway to Slack.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Closed-lost update] --> B[Load deal history]
    B --> C[Extract loss reasons]
    C --> D[Write reusable brief]
    D --> E[Share in Slack]

templates.title: Turn labeled Gmail threads into clean HubSpot contacts
templates.description: Apply one Gmail label and this coworker extracts contact details from the latest message, upserts people in HubSpot, and logs the interaction.
templates.triggerDescription: When a Gmail thread is tagged with the dedicated processing label.
agentInstructions: List the newest message in the labeled Gmail thread.
Extract contact name, email, company, title, phone number, and message context.
Check HubSpot for an existing matching contact before creating anything new.
Upsert the contact record with the best available structured data.
Attach a summary of the interaction to the contact timeline.
Mark the thread or label state to prevent duplicate processing.

summaryBlocks.title: Read the labeled email thread
summaryBlocks.body: Targets only the conversations you explicitly tagged for CRM capture.

summaryBlocks.title: Extract structured contact data
summaryBlocks.body: Pulls the important fields from the latest message and signature.

summaryBlocks.title: Upsert HubSpot cleanly
summaryBlocks.body: Creates or updates the contact and stores a lightweight interaction summary.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Gmail label applied] --> B[Read latest thread message]
    B --> C[Extract contact fields]
    C --> D[Upsert HubSpot contact]
    D --> E[Log interaction summary]

templates.title: Build a daily call list from your company sheet with verified phone numbers
templates.description: Every weekday, this coworker reads unprocessed companies from Google Sheets, finds decision-makers in Apollo, and prepares a call-ready list.
templates.triggerDescription: Run each weekday morning before outbound calling starts.
agentInstructions: Find unprocessed company rows in the source spreadsheet.
Research the best reachable decision-maker and verify role fit.
Enrich the company and contact with confidence notes and source links.
Verify or collect the most reliable phone number available.
Write the final call-ready queue back to the sheet and CRM.
Mark skipped rows with the reason when no valid contact is found.

summaryBlocks.title: Load fresh prospect rows
summaryBlocks.body: Pulls only companies that still need outbound research.

summaryBlocks.title: Enrich for calling
summaryBlocks.body: Finds the right buyer and verifies that the contact is worth dialing.

summaryBlocks.title: Write back a usable queue
summaryBlocks.body: Produces a clean call list with status and contact details.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Daily schedule] --> B[Read pending companies]
    B --> C[Find buyer and phone]
    C --> D[Write call queue]
    D --> E[Mark processed rows]

templates.title: Generate weekly campaign performance reports automatically
templates.description: Every Monday, pull campaign metrics from HubSpot and Google Sheets, generate a summary, and post it to Slack.
templates.triggerDescription: Run every Monday morning for the prior full week.
agentInstructions: Pull the latest campaign metrics from HubSpot and any supporting spreadsheet targets.
Calculate week-over-week changes for spend, leads, pipeline, and conversion metrics.
Highlight campaigns that significantly improved or deteriorated.
Write a concise summary with the top insights and actions.
Post the report to Slack and optionally update the reporting sheet.

summaryBlocks.title: Collect campaign metrics
summaryBlocks.body: Loads the prior week's source data from CRM and reporting sheets.

summaryBlocks.title: Summarize the movers
summaryBlocks.body: Finds the biggest wins, regressions, and efficiency changes.

summaryBlocks.title: Share the weekly digest
summaryBlocks.body: Posts the final report to Slack so the team starts the week aligned.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Monday schedule] --> B[Load campaign metrics]
    B --> C[Calculate changes]
    C --> D[Write weekly summary]
    D --> E[Post to Slack]

templates.title: Score and enrich every new inbound lead instantly
templates.description: When a new contact lands in HubSpot, enrich their profile with LinkedIn data and assign a lead score.
templates.triggerDescription: When an inbound lead is created or promoted into the marketing queue.
agentInstructions: Watch for newly created inbound contacts in HubSpot.
Research the person and company on LinkedIn to validate role, team, and size.
Assign a transparent lead score based on fit and intent signals available in the record.
Write the score rationale and enrichment back to HubSpot.
Notify Slack only when the lead exceeds a high-priority threshold.

summaryBlocks.title: Pick up the new lead
summaryBlocks.body: Starts as soon as a new inbound contact arrives in CRM.

summaryBlocks.title: Enrich and score the record
summaryBlocks.body: Adds firmographic context and a defendable lead score.

summaryBlocks.title: Escalate the best leads
summaryBlocks.body: Sends Slack alerts only when the lead is ready for fast follow-up.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[New lead] --> B[Research person and company]
    B --> C[Assign lead score]
    C --> D[Update HubSpot]
    D --> E[Alert Slack if hot]

templates.title: Alert your team the moment a customer shows churn signals
templates.description: Monitor usage data and support tickets to detect churn risk, then notify the customer success team in Slack with context.
templates.triggerDescription: Run daily across active accounts to flag fresh churn risk.
agentInstructions: Review recent product usage, support volume, renewal timing, and account notes.
Detect churn risk patterns such as drops in usage, repeated complaints, or stalled onboarding.
Summarize the reason for the alert in plain language.
Include the recommended next action for the customer success owner.
Post the alert to Slack and optionally email the owner when the risk is severe.

summaryBlocks.title: Scan for customer risk
summaryBlocks.body: Checks recent account behavior and support signals for change.

summaryBlocks.title: Explain why the account is risky
summaryBlocks.body: Turns noisy signals into a short reasoned narrative.

summaryBlocks.title: Alert the owning team
summaryBlocks.body: Posts a contextual Slack warning with suggested follow-up.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Daily scan] --> B[Review account signals]
    B --> C[Detect churn patterns]
    C --> D[Draft owner guidance]
    D --> E[Send Slack alert]

templates.title: Send personalized onboarding sequences to new customers
templates.description: When a deal closes in Salesforce, kick off a tailored onboarding email sequence with milestones and internal coordination.
templates.triggerDescription: When a new customer opportunity moves into a won stage.
agentInstructions: Detect a newly won deal and collect implementation context from Salesforce.
Determine the onboarding path based on plan, use case, and owner notes.
Draft the welcome email and first milestone sequence in Gmail.
Create an internal Slack handoff with implementation context and deadlines.
Track the onboarding kickoff status on the account or opportunity.

summaryBlocks.title: Start from the won deal
summaryBlocks.body: Uses the CRM close-won event as the trigger for onboarding.

summaryBlocks.title: Personalize the kickoff
summaryBlocks.body: Builds onboarding messages around customer plan and goals.

summaryBlocks.title: Coordinate the internal team

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Deal won] --> B[Load account context]
    B --> C[Prepare onboarding sequence]
    C --> D[Send welcome email]
    D --> E[Notify internal team]

templates.title: Reconcile invoices with payments every week
templates.description: Every Friday, match incoming payments against open invoices in Google Sheets and flag discrepancies for review.
templates.triggerDescription: Run every Friday after the latest payment exports land.
agentInstructions: Load the open invoice ledger and the latest incoming payment records.
Match payments to invoices using invoice ids, amount, customer, and date windows.
Flag overpayments, underpayments, and unmatched transactions.
Write a reconciliation summary and update the tracking sheet.
Send any discrepancies to Slack and optionally email finance owners.

summaryBlocks.title: Load invoice and payment data
summaryBlocks.body: Reads the latest ledgers from the finance spreadsheet and inbox.

summaryBlocks.title: Match the records
summaryBlocks.body: Finds clean matches and isolates the exceptions worth review.

summaryBlocks.title: Report discrepancies
summaryBlocks.body: Publishes a concise list of items finance still needs to resolve.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD

    B --> C[Match records]
    C --> D[Update reconciliation sheet]
    D --> E[Alert on discrepancies]

templates.title: Enrich candidate profiles as soon as they apply
templates.description: When a new application arrives, pull the candidate's LinkedIn profile and prepare a brief for the hiring manager.
templates.triggerDescription: When an application or recruiting inbox message arrives.
agentInstructions: Capture the new candidate details and resume or source link.
Research the candidate's background, relevant roles, and public profile.
Summarize fit for the job opening using concise hiring-manager language.
Write the brief into Notion or the recruiting workspace.
Include notable risks, strengths, and interview topics.

summaryBlocks.body: Starts from the inbound candidate message or application event.

summaryBlocks.title: Research the candidate
summaryBlocks.body: Builds fast context around role history and relevant experience.

summaryBlocks.title: Publish the hiring brief

templates.title: Post GitHub incident alerts to Slack with full context
templates.description: When a critical issue is opened on GitHub, gather related pull requests and recent commits, then post a rich summary to Slack.
templates.triggerDescription: When a new incident or high-priority issue is opened in GitHub.
agentInstructions: Watch for newly created GitHub issues tagged as incidents or critical priority.
Collect issue details, assignee, labels, recent commits, and linked pull requests.
Summarize probable blast radius, ownership, and latest code changes.
Format a concise incident context note for the response channel.
Post the alert to Slack with links back to the issue and related PRs.

summaryBlocks.title: Capture the incident issue
summaryBlocks.body: Starts the moment a critical GitHub issue appears.

summaryBlocks.title: Pull the latest code context
summaryBlocks.body: Finds recent pull requests and commits most likely tied to the incident.

summaryBlocks.title: Publish a response-ready alert
summaryBlocks.body: Posts the situation summary to Slack with the links responders need first.

templates.mermaid:
flowchart TD
    A[Critical issue opened] --> B[Collect issue details]
    B --> C[Find recent PRs and commits]
    C --> D[Draft incident summary]
    D --> E[Send Slack alert]

templates.title: Compile a daily ops standup from all your tools
templates.description: Every morning, pull updates from Linear, Slack, and Google Sheets into a structured standup summary.
templates.triggerDescription: Run every weekday morning before the ops team check-in.
agentInstructions: Load yesterday's updates from Linear, Slack, and the relevant operations sheet.
Group work into shipped, blocked, and at-risk buckets.
Highlight items that need escalation or owner clarification.
Write a concise standup summary using a consistent structure.
Post it to Slack for the daily meeting.

summaryBlocks.title: Collect updates from core systems
summaryBlocks.body: Pulls status changes and discussions from the tools the ops team already uses.

summaryBlocks.title: Structure the standup
summaryBlocks.body: Turns scattered updates into a quick read with clear status buckets.

summaryBlocks.title: Share before the meeting
summaryBlocks.body: Posts the summary to Slack so the team enters the standup aligned.

    B --> C[Group by status]

templates.title: Summarize unread emails into a morning briefing
templates.description: Every morning, read unread emails from the last 24 hours and send a clean digest with action items.
templates.triggerDescription: Run each morning against unread email from the last 24 hours.
agentInstructions: Find unread emails received in the last 24 hours.
Cluster the messages by urgency, sender, and topic.
Summarize only the items worth acting on today.
List concrete next actions and who each email is from.
Send the digest to the user in a compact morning briefing format.

summaryBlocks.title: Review unread mail
summaryBlocks.body: Looks only at fresh unread email so the digest stays short and useful.

summaryBlocks.title: Extract priorities and actions
summaryBlocks.body: Separates important email from noise and identifies what needs a reply.

summaryBlocks.title: Deliver the morning briefing
summaryBlocks.body: Produces a clear inbox digest with action items for the day.

flowchart TD
    A[Morning schedule] --> B[List unread emails]
    B --> C[Rank by priority]
    C --> D[Write digest]
    D --> E[Send briefing]
